definition: The path factor of bodily action aligned with harmlessness and ethical restraint.
notes: The project keeps right action as the default because it is already readable and well established. Appropriate action can be useful in explanatory prose where the emphasis falls on action fitting the path rather than on a static moral label. Ven. Dr. M. Punnaji uses harmonious action in his eightfold-way teaching materials; the repository records that as a source-specific pedagogical alternate. The Dhammarato quotes draft functions as an OSF house-style authority and is recorded here where it uses a stable path-factor heading idiom even when the repository does not adopt that heading as the default standalone rendering.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose emphasizing what conduct accords with the path
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when it improves clarity.

context_rules.context: In Punnaji-style pedagogical exposition of the eightfold path
context_rules.notes: Source-backed alternate, not the house default.

context_rules.context: In Dhammarato quote-book headings or close OSF pedagogical paraphrase
context_rules.notes: Source-backed heading style retained for close alignment with the quotes draft.
